page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and specialized execution capabilities. The instance achieved a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 61947.26 in stress-ng and a strong Passmark CPU score of 56826.42. In memory-intensive and database-oriented tests, it demonstrated a strong score of 26144.88 for Passmark database operations, alongside a static web serving throughput of 43.44 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1688.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1893.9, fall within the average range.

The high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation make this instance class qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise workloads, though the DDR4 memory generation and average single-core speeds represent a tradeoff compared to newer DDR5-based compute-optimized instances. The inclusion of a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD provides high-speed local scratch space, reducing the need for external storage attachments for temporary database operations. This server is configured for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 2048.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB. The hardware configuration includes a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrated strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61947.26 and a Passmark database operations score of 26144.88, while single-core metrics remained average. This resource profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, data analytics, and heavy database operations, where massive RAM capacity and fast local storage are prioritized over raw single-threaded CPU speed.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring two terabytes of RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and high-speed local NVMe storage for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz.
2048.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core.
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed scratch space.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Strong multi-core performance and database operations in benchmark testing.
Designed for in-memory databases, large-scale analytics, and enterprise applications.
